message:
fix(orchestrator): capture adaptive prediction during calibration fallback (bd-k87e)

Fixes bd-k87e: the adaptive-router prediction outcome was only recorded
when the adaptive backend order was actually used. When the calibration
score fell below `min_calibration` and the orchestrator fell back to
the static priority order, the predicted (top_backend, predicted_success)
tuple was thrown away, so calibration telemetry had a blind spot
exactly where we most need it — failed adaptive predictions.

src/orchestrator.rs (`execute_backend`)
- Rework the `backend_order` resolution so it returns a
  `(Option<Vec<Backend>>, Option<(Backend, f64)>)` tuple carrying
  both the backend order (None => use static priority) and the
  adaptive prediction (None => no adaptive evaluation happened).
- Capture the adaptive prediction *before* the
  `calibration_score < min_calibration` check, so even when the
  adaptive order is discarded we still record:
    * `top_backend = selection.recommended_order[0]`
    * `predicted_success = router_state_snapshot().metrics_for(top_backend).success_rate`
      (defaulting to 0.5 when no router state is available yet).
- The three arms now produce:
    * routing_safe_mode: `(None, None)` — no adaptive evaluation.
    * low calibration score: `(None, Some(prediction))` — static
      order, but telemetry records the would-have-been prediction.
    * calibrated adaptive: `(Some(order), Some(prediction))`.
- Removes the now-redundant post-hoc `adaptive_prediction` re-derivation
  that used `backend_order.as_ref().map(...)`; the prediction is now
  an explicit return value from the decision block, so we no longer
  lose it on the fallback path.

No behavior change on the happy path (calibrated adaptive order wins).
The only observable difference is richer calibration telemetry in the
fallback case, which is exactly what bd-k87e asked for.
